# dPlat by DebtNext

dPlat is recovery management SaaS. It is the system of record for the third-party placement lifecycle: routing delinquent and charged-off accounts to outside agencies, law firms, and recovery vendors, then tracking placements, results, disputes, and compliance across the whole network from one platform.

DebtNext, LLC built dPlat and has run it since 2003. DebtNext is a Transworld Systems Inc. company.

## Who it's for

Credit originators who place accounts with a network of outside recovery vendors: utilities, financial services, telecom, fintech, insurance, and healthcare or revenue-cycle teams. dPlat is an enterprise platform, sold by demo and configured per portfolio. dPlat works with any collection agency, law firm, or recovery vendor already in a client's network.

## What it does

- Vendor placement and assignment strategy across treatment tiers
- Optimization that shifts volume toward vendors who recover more
- Issue, dispute, and complaint handling with SLA timers and audit trails
- Compliance workflows for FDCPA, Reg F, FCRA, TCPA, and SCRA
- Reporting and dashboards for liquidation, cost, net-back, and vendor performance
- Integration with servicing, billing, ERP, CIS, and recovery systems

## Key pages

- [Home](https://debtnext.com/): what dPlat is and who runs recovery on it.
- [Platform](https://debtnext.com/platform): the modules that make up dPlat, in one place.
- [Placement](https://debtnext.com/platform/placement): the decision engine that routes accounts to vendors across treatment tiers.
- [Optimization](https://debtnext.com/platform/optimization): how placement volume shifts toward higher-performing vendors.
- [Issues](https://debtnext.com/platform/issues): dispute, complaint, and exception handling with SLA timers and audit trails.
- [Reporting](https://debtnext.com/platform/reporting): reports and dashboards for liquidation, cost, net-back, and vendor performance.
- [Integrations](https://debtnext.com/platform/integrations): how dPlat connects to servicing, billing, ERP, and recovery systems.
- [Solutions](https://debtnext.com/solutions): the industries dPlat runs recovery for.
- [Utilities](https://debtnext.com/solutions/utilities): final-bill placement, deposit handling, and PUC-ready compliance.
- [Financial services](https://debtnext.com/solutions/financial-services): card, installment, line-of-credit, and overdraft recovery with Reg F workflows.
- [Telecom](https://debtnext.com/solutions/telecom): high-volume post-disconnect recovery with short treatment windows.
- [Fintech](https://debtnext.com/solutions/fintech): API-first recovery for direct lenders, BNPL, and digital banking.
- [Insurance](https://debtnext.com/solutions/insurance): subrogation, deductible recovery, salvage, and premium receivables.
- [Healthcare](https://debtnext.com/solutions/healthcare): patient-responsibility balances and bad-debt placement for RCM teams.
- [Why dPlat](https://debtnext.com/why-dplat): when to buy recovery software instead of building it or stretching spreadsheets.
- [Compare](https://debtnext.com/compare): how dPlat compares to NeuAnalytics, Convoke, Imagine Cloud, Symend, and HighRadius.
- [About](https://debtnext.com/company/about): DebtNext, its focus on one product, and its Transworld Systems Inc. ownership.
- [Request a demo](https://debtnext.com/demo): a 30-minute walkthrough scoped to a portfolio and vendor mix.
